content: A group of 9 friction piles are arranged in a square grid maintaining equal spacing in all directions. Each pile is of diameter 300 mm and length 7 m. Assume that the soil is cohesionless with effective friction angle 𝜙′ = 32°. What is the center-to-center spacing of the piles (in m) for the pile group efficiency of 60%?
explanation:
Given the parameters :

  • Number of piles, $ N = 9 $
  • Diameter of each pile, $ D = 300 \, \text{mm} $
  • Length of each pile, $ L = 7 \, \text{m} $
  • Soil type: cohesionless with $ \phi = 32^\circ $
  • Group efficiency, $ n_g = 60\% $

To find the center-to-center spacing $ S $ of the piles for the pile group efficiency of 60%, follow these steps :

Group efficiency of pile $( \eta_g )$ is given by :

$ \eta_g = \frac{Q_{ug}}{N Q_{up}} $

where :

  • $ Q_{ug} $ is the ultimate group capacity
  • $ Q_{up} $ is the ultimate capacity of a single pile
  • $ \frac{1}{2}(\gamma L) \tan \delta(2 s+d) l \times 4 $ is the combined contribution of the piles

On simplifying, we get :

$ 0.6 = \frac{ \frac{1}{2} (\gamma L) \tan \delta (2s + d) l \times 4 }{ 9 \times \frac{1}{2} (\gamma L) \tan \delta (\pi d l) } $

$ 0.6 = \frac{ (2s + d) \times 4 }{ 9 \times \pi d } $

Solving for $ S $ :

$ S = \frac{1}{2} \left[ \frac{0.6 \times 9 \times \pi \times 0.3}{4} - 0.3 \right] = 0.486 \, \text{m} $

Therefore, the correct center-to-center spacing of the piles is 0.486 meters.

content:
A square concrete pile of 10 m length is driven into a deep layer of uniform homogenous clay. Average unconfined compressive strength of the clay, determined through laboratory tests on undisturbed samples extracted from the clay layer, is 100 kPa. If the ultimate compressive load capacity of the driven pile is 632 kN, the required width of the pile is __________ mm. (in integer)

Bearing capacity factor Nc = 9; adhesion factor $$\alpha$$ = 0.7)
explanation:
Qup = qbAb + qsAs

Cu = $${{{q_u}} \over 2}$$ = $${{100} \over 2}$$ = 50 kN/m2

= 9 $$\times$$ CB2 + $$\alpha$$$$\overline C $$(4BL)

632 kN = 9 $$\times$$ 50 $$\times$$ B2 + 0.7 $$\times$$ 50 (4B $$\times$$ 10)

B = 0.4 m

B = 400 mm

content: A raft foundation of 30 m $$\times$$ 25 m is proposed to be constructed at a depth of 8 m in a sand layer. A 25 m thick saturated clay layer exists 2 m below the base of the raft foundation. Below the clay layer, a dense sand layer exists at the site. A 25 mm thick undisturbed sample was collected from the mid-depth of the clay layer and tested in a laboratory oedometer under double drainage condition. It was found that the soil sample had undergone 50% consolidation settlement in 10 minutes. The time (in days) required for 25% consolidation settlement of the raft foundation will be ___________. (round off to the nearest integer)
explanation:
Given: $ H = 25 \text{ mm} $

For double drainage :

$ d = \frac{H_0}{2} = \frac{25}{2} = 12.5 \text{ mm} $

For $ U = 50\% $ :

$ T_v = \frac{\pi}{4} \times 0.5^2 = 0.196 $

The coefficient of consolidation ($ C_v $) is calculated as :

$ T_v = \frac{C_v t}{d^2} $

$ C_v = \frac{T_v d^2}{t} = \frac{0.196 \times 12.5^2 \times 10^{-6}}{\frac{10}{60 \times 24}} $

Field scenario :

$ H_0 = 25 \text{ m} $

$ d = 12.5 \text{ m} $

For $ U = 25\% $ :

$ T_v = \frac{\pi}{4} \times 0.25^2 = 0.49 $

Now, solving for $ t $:

$ \frac{12.5^2 \times 0.49}{t} = \frac{0.196 \times 12.5^2 \times 10^{-6}}{\frac{10}{60 \times 24}} $

$ t = 1736.11 \text{ days} $

Therefore, the time required for 25% consolidation settlement of the raft foundation is approximately 1736 days.

content: A $4 \times 4$ group pile, with each pile 20 m long and 500 mm in diameter, is installed in a square pattern in a clayey soil, as shown in the figure. The average unconfined compressive strength of the soil is $100 \mathrm{kN} / \mathrm{m}^2$, and the adhesion factor is 0.8 . Neglect the bearing at the tip of the piles. For a group efficiency factor of 1.0 , the centre to centre spacing (s) of the piles (in m ) would be ________ (round off to two decimal places).
explanation:
The problem involves calculating the center-to-center spacing, $ s $, of piles in a $ 4 \times 4 $ square pile group installed in clayey soil. Here is the explanation detailing the steps and calculations:

Group Efficiency Concept:

The group efficiency, $\eta_g$, is defined as the ratio of the average group capacity, $ Q_{\text{avg}} $, to the total single pile capacity multiplied by the number of piles, $ nQ_u $. Given that the group efficiency factor is 1.0, we have:

$ \eta_g = \frac{Q_{\text{avg}}}{nQ_u} = 1 $

Calculate Cohesion Factor:

The unconfined compressive strength (UCS) of the soil is 100 kN/m². The cohesion $ \bar{C} $ is half of the UCS:

$ \bar{C} = \frac{\text{UCS}}{2} = \frac{100}{2} = 50 \, \text{kN/m}^2 $

Capacity of the Group:

The total capacity of the pile group, $ Q_{ug} $, is given by the product of the number of piles and the capacity of a single pile:

$ Q_{ug} = nQ_{up} $

Determine Pile Spacing:

Using the formula for the group capacity considering adhesion:

$ \bar{C}(4 \times B \times L) = n[\alpha \bar{C}(\pi D \times L)] $

Substitute the known values:

$ 50 \times 4 \times B \times 20 = 16 \times [0.8 \times 50 \times \pi \times 0.5 \times 20] $

Solve for $ B $:

Calculating the above equation:

$ 50 \times 4 \times B \times 20 = 16 \times [0.8 \times 50 \times \pi \times 0.5 \times 20] $

This simplifies to:

$ B = 5.026 \, \text{m} $

Relate $ B $ to Spacing $ s $:

Considering the relation $ B = 3s + D $, where $ D = 0.5 \, \text{m} $:

$ B = 3s + 0.5 = 5.026 $

Solving for $ s $, we find:

$ s = 1.51 \, \text{m} $

Hence, the center-to-center spacing of the piles is approximately 1.51 meters.

content:
A single pile with 450 mm diameter has been driven into a homogeneous clay layer, which has an undrained cohesion ( $\mathrm{c}_{\mathrm{u}}$ ) of 20 kPa and unit weight of $18 \mathrm{kN} / \mathrm{m}^3$. The ground water table is found to be at the surface of the clay layer. The adhesion factor ( $\alpha$ ) of the soil is 0.95 and bearing capacity factor $\left(N_c\right)$ is 9 . The pile is supporting a column load of 144 kN with a factor of safety of 3.0 against ultimate axial pile capacity in compression.

The required embedment depth of the pile (in m ) is _________ (rounded off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
To determine the required embedment depth of a pile driven into a clay layer, we must analyze the pile's load capacity, taking into account the provided soil parameters and pile dimensions.

Given:

Pile Diameter: 450 mm

Undrained Cohesion, $ c_u $: 20 kPa

Unit Weight of Clay: 18 kN/m³

Ground Water Table: At surface level

Adhesion Factor, $ \alpha $: 0.95

Bearing Capacity Factor, $ N_c $: 9

Column Load Supported by Pile: 144 kN

Factor of Safety (FOS): 3.0 against ultimate axial pile capacity in compression

Calculation:

Ultimate Load Capacity of the Pile, $ Q_{\text{up}} $:

The ultimate load capacity can be computed by combining both the base resistance and skin friction:

$ Q_{\text{up}} = N_c \cdot c_u \cdot A_b + \alpha \cdot \bar{C} \cdot A_s $

Base Area, $ A_b $:

$ A_b = \frac{\pi}{4} \cdot (0.45)^2 $

Surface Area, $ A_s $ (for skin friction):

$ A_s = \pi \cdot 0.45 \cdot L $

Substituting values:

$ Q_{\text{up}} = 9 \cdot 20 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4} \cdot (0.45)^2 + 0.95 \cdot 20 \cdot (\pi \cdot 0.45 \cdot L) $

Safe Load Capacity, $ Q_{\text{safe}} $:

The safe load capacity is obtained by dividing the ultimate capacity by the factor of safety:

$ Q_{\text{safe}} = \frac{Q_{\text{up}}}{\text{FOS}} $

Given:

$ Q_{\text{safe}} = 144 \times 3 = 432 \text{ kN} $

Solving for $ L $:

Rearranging and solving:

$ 432 = 28.627 + 26.861L $

$ L = \frac{432 - 28.627}{26.861} = 15.01 \text{ m} $

Conclusion:

The required embedment depth of the pile is approximately 15 m when rounded to the nearest integer.

content:
A group of total 16 piles are arranged in a square grid format. The center-to-center spacing (s) between adjacent piles is 3 m. The diameter (d) and length of embedment of each pile are 1 m and 20 m, respectively. The design capacity of each pile is 1000 kN in the vertical downward direction. The pile group efficiency $${\eta _g}$$ is given by

$${\eta _g} = 1 - {\theta \over {90}}\left[ {{{(n - 1)m + (m - 1)n} \over {mn}}} \right]$$

where m and n are number of rows and columns in the plan grid of pile arrangement and $$\theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{d \over s}} \right)$$.

The design value of the pile group capacity (in kN) in the vertical downward direction is _________. (round off to the nearest integer)
explanation:
Given :

Number of piles = 16

Center-to-center spacing between piles (s) = 3 m

Diameter of each pile (d) = 1 m

Length of embedment of each pile = 20 m

Design capacity of each pile = 1000 kN in the vertical downward direction

We have :

Arrangement: $N = 16$ (4 × 4 pattern)

Calculate $\theta$ :

$ \theta = \tan^{-1} \left( \frac{d}{s} \right) $

$ \theta = \tan^{-1} \left( \frac{1}{3} \right) $

$ \theta = 18.435^\circ $

Calculate the pile group efficiency $\eta_g$ :

$ \eta_g = 1 - \frac{\theta}{90} \left[ \frac{(n - 1)m + (m - 1)n}{mn} \right] $

For a 4 × 4 pile arrangement, $m = 4$ and $n = 4$ :

$ \eta_g = 1 - \frac{18.435}{90} \left[ \frac{(4 - 1)4 + (4 - 1)4}{4 \times 4} \right] $

$ \eta_g = 1 - \frac{18.435}{90} \left[ \frac{12 + 12}{16} \right] $

$ \eta_g = 1 - \frac{18.435}{90} \left[ 1.5 \right] $

$ \eta_g = 0.6925 $

Calculate the design value of the pile group capacity $Q_{ug}$ :

$ \eta_g = \frac{Q_{ug}}{N Q_{up}} $

$ 0.6925 = \frac{Q_{ug}}{16 \times 1000} $

$ Q_{ug} = 0.6925 \times 16000 $

$ Q_{ug} = 11080 \text{ kN} $

So, the design value of the pile group capacity in the vertical downward direction is approximately $11080$ kN.
